articles.title: NIELSEN: SPORTS CONTINUES TO PROP UP AD-SUPPORTED TV
articles.summary: Sports remain the main driver of ad-supported TV. It accounted for nearly 30% of total viewership in Q4 2025, compared with 9.8% for broadcast TV without sports and 18% for non-sport cable. Audiences 18-49 rely heavily on ad-supported sources, with 66.7% of that viewing coming from streaming platforms, mostly on YouTube, Hulu, and Amazon Prime. Live events like the Super Bowl LX, which drew 125.6M viewers, and expanded MLB sponsorships show sports continue to deliver a large and commercially valuable audience.

articles.title: SHORT-FORM VIDEO IS DRIVING VIEWERS TO WATCH FULL TV SHOWS
articles.summary: Short-form videos on social platforms are increasingly functioning as a discovery mechanism for longer-form television and film, especially among younger viewers. 85% of 16-24 year olds watch short-form weekly, with 69% daily, and 87% of them reported watching full programmes after seeing clips. Social discovery is strongest among this age group, with 49% finding shows via social platforms and trending audio influencing half of them. Meanwhile, older viewers are less affected by social content.

articles.title: INVISIBLE PROMPTS: WHY YOU CAN'T SEE THE AI SEARCHES DRIVING YOUR LEADS
articles.summary: AI search is driving leads through ‘invisible prompts'. These are private, highly personalized conversations shaped by a user's context, not just what is typed. This makes traditional keyword tracking and optimization far less useful, since brands cannot see or replicate the exact inputs behind these recommendations. Instead of chasing specific prompts, the focus should be on creating detailed, use case–driven content that teaches AI when and why to make recommendations. Over time, this builds stronger visibility at the topic level and works more like digital word of mouth, where the depth of content determines whether a brand appears.

articles.title: 7 ORGANIC CONTENT INVESTMENTS THAT DRIVE ECOMMERCE ROI
articles.summary: Ecommerce ROI from organic content relies on structured data and multi-platform discovery rather than publishing large volumes of content. AI-generated summaries reduce clicks, so content must be authoritative enough for AI to cite and trustworthy for shoppers. Social search is essential, with 86% of Gen Z using TikTok weekly for searches. Visual discovery is also growing, with Google Lens handling over 100 billion visual searches in 2025, 20% of which were product related. Brands should focus on strong product pages, structured product data, reviews and UGC, and decision support guides. Low-value, scaled SEO blog content and other shortcuts should be avoided.

articles.title: THE AHREFS API IS NOW AVAILABLE ON ALL PAID PLANS
articles.summary: API access is now a feature in all paid Ahrefs plans, not just the Enterprise tier. SEOs can use the API for scaled analysis, MCP integration, custom tools, and more. Row limits vary by plan tier (Lite: 25,000, Standard: 150,000, Advanced: 500,000).

articles.title: SHOPIFY IS PREPARING FOR AI SHOPPING AGENTS TO CHANGE EVERYTHING
articles.summary: Shopify believes that AI agents will act as merit-based personal shoppers, surfacing products based on individual preferences rather than retailer dominance in search results. It is building an AI merchant assistant called Sidekick alongside a support agent and a protocol for agents to read merchant product data. With only 18% of US retail purchases happening online, the company sees agentic commerce as a major discovery channel especially for smaller and long-tail merchants.

articles.title: HOW MR. CLEAN CAN DOMINATE SOCIAL MEDIA THROUGH MASCOT-LED MARKETING
articles.summary: Mr. Clean is a culturally iconic brand with underused social potential. Its mascot has driven attention through a Super Bowl ad and a 2026 retirement stunt, yet content focuses on products rather than personality. A character-led strategy would give Mr. Clean a humorous voice, make social channels his main platform, and engage younger audiences. Campaigns could feature TikTok reactions to messy videos, structured Magic Eraser UGC, and meme-driven content to turn the mascot into a living, ongoing brand presence.

articles.title: HOW RETAILERS CAN LEVERAGE THEIR PRIVATE LABEL MARKETING ADVANTAGE OVER NATIONAL BRANDS
articles.summary: Private label sales reached a record $283B last year, with retailers expanding well beyond budget options into premium tiers to capture a wider range of shoppers. Retailers hold a built-in marketing edge at the point of sale, where they can merchandise private labels alongside national brands, dedicate separate store sections, and use loyalty programs and targeted promotions to drive trials. They are also shortening innovation cycles by leveraging shopper data to quickly follow trends and launch new products.

articles.title: 7 RETENTION HILLS TO DIE ON
articles.summary: Retention fails when brands ignore early churn, rely on discounts, and lack strong LTV CAC fundamentals.
